channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Prompt Security adds code sanitization, data leak prevention for GitHub Copilot
item.description: Prompt Security has announced an upgrade to its security and governance platform for GitHub Copilot and other AI coding assistants.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japanese EV automaker ZM Trucks plants its US base in California
item.description: The company said it will use that facility to manufacture a range of zero-emission products including electric commercial trucks, tractors, and airport ground service equipment.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Gowen, The Mercury News

item.title: iPhone users turn on to DeepSeek AI
item.description: As if from nowhere, OpenAI competitor DeepSeek has somersaulted to the top of the iPhone App Store chart, overtaking ChatGPT’s OpenAI.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple's Beats Powerbeats Pro 2 Confirmed for 2025 with Heart Rate Monitoring Tech
item.description: Apple's Beats Powerbeats Pro 2 have been confirmed for 2025, featuring heart rate monitoring technology and connectivity to gym equipment.
item.dc:creator: matt.evans@futurenet.com (Matt Evans), TechRadar

item.title: Scammers find sneaky way to bypass your iPhone's safety features
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says cybercriminals are exploiting iMessage phishing protection, tricking users to reactivate links.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 14 things keeping IT leaders up at night
item.description: There’s a lot for CIOs to stress about these days — from longstanding concerns about uptime and availability to the quantum revolution on the horizon. Of course, there are plenty of other issues worrying CIOs here and now, too.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: SAP Customers Face Deadline to Migrate to S/4HANA
item.description: SAP customers face deadline to migrate to S/4HANA, SAP considering extension of maintenance window, Rise with SAP program offers transition option
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Sweden seizes vessel suspected of 'sabotage' after undersea data cable rupture in Baltic Sea
item.description: RIGA, Latvia (AP) — Swedish prosecutors announced Sunday night that they have opened a preliminary investigation into suspected aggravated “sabotage” and ordered the detention of a vessel in the Baltic Sea suspected of damaging an underwater fiber optic cable connecting Latvia and the Swedish island
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A new OnePlus Open 2 tease shows just how thin the upcoming foldable will be
item.description: The latest promotional image shared by Oppo puts its upcoming foldable up against the Apple iPad Pro.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Presumed Innocent season 2: everything we know so far about the Apple TV Plus show's return
item.description: Here's the latest news and rumors surrounding Presumed Innocent season 2, which will air on Apple TV Plus.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Puppet open source fork OpenVox arrives
item.description: The Vox Pupuli collective group has announced the first release of OpenVox, a community-maintained open source implementation of the Puppet configuration management system.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Trump’s move to lift Biden-era AI rules sparks debate over fast-tracked advances
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive order removing Biden-Administration rules governing AI development is being cast as an opening of AI development flood gates, which could fast track advances in the still-new technology, but could also pose risks.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: More questions than answers around Trump’s Stargate AI plans
item.description: In its second day of operation, the Trump administration announced a $500 billion private sector initiative called Stargate intending to build AI infrastructure across the United States and create over 100,000 U.S. jobs. But questions about the project abound. Pres.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: California lab makes new discovery in search for HIV vaccine
item.description: A unique reaction in which antibodies bind to other antibodies may help scientists at Scripps Research better understand how to design a vaccine
item.dc:creator: Ashley Mackin Solomon, The Mercury News

item.title: Trump AI project presents Musk with early test of loyalty
item.description: Elon Musk’s criticism of an artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure project backed by President Trump is presenting an early test of how the SpaceX CEO will balance his personal conflicts in the technology space with the interests of the president.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: El 29% de los CDO no ven futuro en el puesto
item.description: Casi un tercio de los líderes de TI que ocupan el puesto de director de datos y funciones similares en empresas de la lista Fortune 1000 no creen en el futuro a largo plazo del puesto de CDO.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Ready or not, here it comes: GenAI in 2025
item.description: If you’re a software developer right now, it is nearly impossible to avoid chatter about generative AI. You’re probably already using AI tools in your work, whether you like it or not. Some of your normie friends are using ChatGPT as a search engine, with hilarious and alarming results.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok France is being sued by 7 families. Here’s why
item.description: In the moment when her world shattered three years ago, Stephanie Mistre found her 15-year-old daughter, Marie, lifeless in the bedroom where she died by suicide.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: The sickening truth: Healthcare data breaches reach all-time high
item.description: Healthcare records for more than 184 million Americans were breached in 2024, but you can take steps to minimize that risk. Kurt the CyberGuy explains.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tips on overcoming the loss of cherished, personal belongings in disasters
item.description: Losing important sentimental belongings — those items that represented who you are — can be traumatic for those who go through disasters that destroy homes.
item.dc:creator: JULIA RUBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pakistan's parliament passes bill with sweeping controls on social media
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Pakistan’s lower house of parliament on Thursday passed a controversial bill that will give the government sweeping controls on social media, including sending users to prison for spreading disinformation.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Screen breaks and the right desk setup offer relief from work-related eye strain
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The trouble started every day at around 3 p.m., after Cathy Higgins had spent five or six hours staring at an array of computer screens at her desk. Her university job overseeing research projects involved peering closely at numbers and details on contracts, applications and budgets.
item.dc:creator: CATHY BUSSEWITZ, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to delete Facebook, Instagram and Threads if you don't like Meta's changes
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Should I stay or should I go — from Meta's social media platforms? That's what some Facebook, Instagram and Threads users are wondering after Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg's announcement this month that the company is relaxing rules on harmful content such as hate speech and abandoning its
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: This flying motorcycle can take you from traffic to sky in minutes
item.description: The Skyrider X1 from Rictor claims to be the "first amphibious flying passenger motorcycle," and it combines land and air travel in one sleek design.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration shuts down White House Spanish-language page and social media
item.description: Within hours of President Donald Trump’s inauguration, the new administration took down the Spanish-language version of the official White House website. The site — currently https://www.whitehouse.gov/es/ — now gives users an “Error 404” message.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Stargate Project launched for OpenAI AI infrastructure
item.description: In a venture unveiled by Pres. Donald Trump on January 21, companies including SoftBank, Oracle, OpenAI, and MGX are funding a new AI company, called the Stargate Project, to invest $500 billion to build AI infrastructure for OpenAI in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI experiment in halfpipe judging at X Games will give snowboarders a glimpse into the future
item.description: The X Games will experiment judging halfpipe runs this week in Aspen using artificial intelligence, the cutting-edge technology that could someday play a role in the way subjectively judged sports are scored.
item.dc:creator: EDDIE PELLS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Auto-followed Trump on Facebook? It's probably because you previously liked the POTUS account
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Some Facebook and Instagram users were surprised to find themselves automatically following the accounts of President Donald Trump or Vice President JD Vance this week after the president was inaugurated — but that's likely because they had previously followed official U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, a populist president, is flanked by tech billionaires at his inauguration
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Some of the most exclusive seats at President Donald Trump’s inauguration on Monday were reserved for powerful tech CEOs who also happen to be among the world’s richest men.
item.dc:creator: ALI SWENSON, Associated Press, KRON4
